## ❌ NEVER PROPOSE (hard rejections)

These proposal types are permanently rejected. Proposing them again wastes a swarm slot.

- API gateway / microservices — monolith works (Session 23)
- Redis — not needed until 2+ Railway instances (Session 23)
- ORM (SQLAlchemy, Prisma) — Supabase SDK only (Session 23)
- Privacy by default (hidden scores) — CEO chose public by default (Session 25)
- OpenAI as primary LLM — Gemini is primary, cost decision (Session 25)
- Single-word keywords in questions — proven gameable (Session 42)
- Self-assessment as evidence — circular by definition (Mistake #47, Session 42)

## ✅ HIGH-VALUE PATTERNS (do more of these)

These types of proposals consistently get implemented or praised.

- Specific security vulnerabilities with CVSS score — always gets attention (Session 25, 42)
- Storage/cost math — CTO missed the 14-43GB/year calculation (Session 25)
- User journey gaps — Leyla/Nigar persona reviews catch what code reviews miss (Session 25)
- Concrete file paths — 'fix line 39 in aura.py' > 'fix the auth issue' (Session 25)
- Calibrated estimates — '3 hours, not 3 days' with specific breakdown (Session 25)
- Blind cross-testing methodology — Session 42 proved its value (CEO validated, Session 42)
- GRS analysis on new questions — compute_grs() before proposing any question (Session 42)
